author: Quoia75
msg:
Literally was coming to post about this. I'm half way through and will finish it tomorrow. Jamie did great, and as usual Cal and Rob are gracious hosts.

Jaimie addressed some key questions, such as the results of the floor split on the marketplace and how and when it will be implemented -  within days (not weeks or months). Also, training materials are being developed for UNOs to pass on to ULOs to take some of the heavy lifting off of us as far educating them.

He starts talking about Unity around the 42:00 minute mark.👌

author: T E
msg: Hey I was just thinking about taxes because, 'tis the season. For Unity, I was wondering what the taxable event would be.  I assume that the earning of UPs is not in and of itself a taxable event as UPs are only redeemable for currency.  Would that mean that the conversion of UPs to fiat (when available) would be the taxable event?  Typically buying crypto isn't a taxable event, but if UPs represent earnings, does the conversion to crypto become a taxable event?  Any thoughts on this?

author: Adam Buracchi
msg: I believe that once you redeem whether in crypto/stable or FIAT  is an income taxable event. If you redeem in crypto and swap to stable/FIAT after the chosen  crypto made you a profit or a loss then you will have Capital Gain Tax or Loss. NFA obviously

author: Quoia75

author: Forty41

author: T E
msg: So if I convert UPs > crypto > fiat all in the same day, there could conceivably be no taxes?  1. UPs aren't earnings, 2. Buying/redeeming UPs for crypto isn't a taxable event, 3. Converting a crypto to fiat at the same cost basis wouldn't trigger capital gains.

author: T E
msg: Not trying to get out of anything, but just trying to figure out how it would work in reality.

author: Adam Buracchi
msg:
1. Once Redeem ups is considered income at least is what I will consider in UK which is taxable 
2. Correct 
3.Correct no CGT if same cost basis

author: T E
msg: So alternatively, one could stack UPs, you know to buy the dip or whatever,  and then they could not claim that as income if it suits their tax situation, correct?  Again, I understand, NFA...just curious about it is all...

author: Kerry
msg: Probably as we scale up, right not I'm using the one posted in the software section of the farmers group. You have to use the scrapper then change to .txt file then upload the Unity Reward Tracker so not efficient but works

author: Adam Buracchi
msg: In UK UPs will always be considered income as far as I know 1UPs=1$ so as I redeem whether in crypto,stable or FIAT I will convert dollar value to sterling pounds and consider that income tax

author: T E
msg: If you redeem for crypto, how will that be tracked as income?  I mean, that makes sense for sure, just curious how you would track a conversion to crypto as income when other conversions to crypto would not be considered a taxable event.

author: Adam Buracchi
msg: You can be paid in crypto and still be an income

author: Adam Buracchi
msg: Income is taxable

author: James_WM_ENO
msg: Yes exactly that.. UPs are $ so you pay income tax on all earned UPs

author: Adam Buracchi
msg: They may not be Capital Gain Tax but in UK if you have any type of income paid in crypto you will have to pay taxes… for example I pay income tax on my crypto staking rewards which is paid in crypto

author: T E

author: Forty41
msg: We used to have to get signed agreements to use their names which were often based on performance agreements. I know you work in pharma and when I did a deal with J&J they were hugely protective and if they saw their name used as a reference they were all over me

author: HSL
msg: [Photo] Two drastic valley 👀

author: Forty41
msg: I used to see it quite simply 1. You want use my name then do a good job 2. If you value my brand as a client - what’s it worth 3 . I don’t want the competition to know if I’ve gained advantage by using you. 4 . Mess up and you can’t use me as a reference.  5. I’m really smug and leading the market so go shout about what we are doing but I want preferred pricing . So many differing reasons why it’s not always easy just to name a client .

author: FriendlyUrchin
msg: This is really good. Good insight. Thanks Forty
